What is GPS Sampling

What is GPS Sampling, why it was developed and what are the advantages in using it.

Source: vignettes/what.Rmd
what.Rmd

GPS Sampling is a web application designed for providing users with procedures for estimating population size using geographical information. The application is based on R freeware and the Shiny package.

GPS Sampling is a fork of GeoSampler, originally developed by Epicentre to support the activities of Médecins Sans Frontières. This fork is maintained independently for internal use and released under the WTFPL license.

Please use the issue tracker on GitHub to suggest enhancements or report problems: https://github.com/epicentre-msf/gpssampling/issues.

Three sampling methods are implemented:

  • the Quadrats
  • the T-square
  • the Roof-Simple-Random sampling

The three methods have different limitations and potential weaknesses depending of the context they are applied and the type and quality of information available.

Developed by Yves Amevoin, Claude (Anthropic AI).

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
